full_prompt: Design a single-part, monolithic titanium mounting bracket based on the jet-engine OEM / open-CAD platform Jet Engine Bracket Challenge (2013 open CAD challenge). The bracket connects a jet engine casing to an external accessory and must transfer load without permanent deformation under transient post-FBO (Fan Blade Out) conditions. The objective is to minimize mass while passing four independent static load cases that are baked into the open-CAD challenge page. Geometry: bounding envelope 203 mm x 152 mm x 102 mm (8 x 6 x 4 inches). Top flange has 4x through-holes 12.7 mm (1/2 in) on a 76.2 x 76.2 mm (3 x 3 in) rectangular pattern; bottom carries a 25.4 mm (1 in) pin-hole horizontal. Vertical distance top-to-pin 120 mm. Single monolithic body, no welds/fasteners/inserts. Material Ti-6Al-4V (E = 113.8 GPa, nu = 0.342, yield 860 MPa, ultimate 950 MPa, density 4430 kg/m^3). Four challenge load cases applied as bearing pressure on the pin-hole inner surface with the top-flange bolt holes fully fixed: LC1 vertical 35,586 N (8000 lbf) upward; LC2 horizontal 37,810 N (8500 lbf) along engine axis; LC3 42,258 N (9500 lbf) at 42 deg off-vertical; LC4 torque 5650 N*m about pin axis (5000 lbf * 0.5 ft). Pass/fail (binding numbers): (R1) max von Mises <= ultimate / 1.5 = 633 MPa; (R2) max displacement at pin hole <= 1.0 mm; (R3) final mass <= 1.0 kg AND >= 40% lighter than bulk-block baseline; (R4) buckling first-mode load factor at LC3 >= 2.0. Primary verification is structural FEA (linear static x4 load cases) + linear buckling eigenvalue for LC3. Thermal, fluid, radiation, vibration NOT required. Topology optimization is the outer loop required to reach R3. Deliverables: STEP AP242 + Parasolid, and a report with mass in g, max von Mises per case, max pin-hole displacement per case, first buckling load factor.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic Ti-6Al-4V mounting bracket per the jet-engine OEM open-CAD Jet Engine Bracket Challenge (2013). The bracket connects an engine casing to an external accessory and must transfer load without permanent deformation under transient post-FBO (Fan Blade Out) conditions. Four challenge load cases are applied as bearing pressure on the 1 in (25.4 mm) pin-hole inner surface, with the 4 x 12.7 mm top-flange bolt holes (76.2 x 76.2 mm pattern) fully fixed. Pin-hole displacement <= 1.0 mm per case. Topology optimization is required as the outer loop.

Envelope: bounding 203 x 152 x 102 mm (8 x 6 x 4 in); top-flange-to-pin vertical distance 120 mm.

Load cases.
- LC1 vertical: 35,586 N (8000 lbf) upward.
- LC2 horizontal along engine axis: 37,810 N (8500 lbf).
- LC3 42 deg off-vertical: 42,258 N (9500 lbf).
- LC4 torque about pin axis: 5650 N*m (5000 lbf * 0.5 ft).
- LC5 linear buckling under LC3, first-mode load factor >= 2.0.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Achieving >=40% mass reduction vs bulk block is the GE benchmark; outer topology optimization loop around the structural FEA.

prompt.title: GE Jet Engine Bracket (open-CAD platform open challenge)
prompt.brief: Canonical open-CAD platform bracket; four static load cases + topology optimization for >= 40% mass reduction in Ti-6Al-4V.
